MASTER CALENDAR JURY

Notice Of And Motion For Leave To Cross-Complaint Against Cross Defendant City And County Of San Francisco And Wedriveu
Off Calendar May-05-2026 Off Calendar

Joint Notice Of Settlement And Motion For An Order For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement [C.C.P. 877.6(A)(2)]
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS WEDRIVEU, INC.'S AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S JOINT MOTION FOR AN ORDER FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT [CCP 877.6(a)(2)]

Appearances:
Steven F. Egler of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.3974, steven.egler@sfcityatty.org) for defendant City and County of San Francisco;
Allison Stewart of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (415.362.2580, Allison.Stewart@lewisbrisbois.com) appearing via Zoom for defendant WeDriveU, Inc.
Marc Appell of the Law Office of Marc Appell (818.710.7177, appell@appelllaw.com) appearing via Zoom for plaintiff Peter Allen;
Conrad Wu of the Bryant Law Group, LLP (415.378.4298, cwu@sflaw.net) for defendant Phoenix Holiday, Inc.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants WeDriveU, Inc. and the City and County of San Francisco's opposed motion for a determination of good-faith settlement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 877.6, is granted.

The court has considered the factors set out in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward Clyde Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499, including a rough approximation of the plaintiff's total recovery, a rough approximation of the settling defendant's proportionate liability, the amount paid in settlement, and any evidence of collusion or fraud. The court concludes that the settlement reached by the settling defendants is proportionate to their potential liability at trial such that it is in " 'the ballpark' " (id., at pp. 499-500) of the settling defendants' potential liability in light of their showing that Phoenix has admitted that it was the owner and operator of the bus that discharged Plaintiff into traffic. Even assuming the truth of Phoenix's facts that the City allows commuter shuttles to park at the location where Plaintiff exited the Phoenix bus, or that a City bus struck Plaintiff with its mirror as he exited, the contribution by WeDriveU and the City to an overall assumed $300,000 case value is not so small as to be outside of the ballpark of a good-faith settlement. Moreover, the City and WeDriveU present evidence of their nonliability, including Plaintiff's deposition transcript that his knowledge of a City bus's involvement in the accident is based on hearsay. (Egler Reply Decl., Ex. F, pp. 19-20.) In addition, there is no evidence of any collusion or fraud. The court concludes that the settlement is in good faith. This finding bars all existing, potential, and future claims by, through and against WeDriveU and the City for equitable comparative contribution, or partial or comparative indemnity, based upon comparative negligence or comparative fault. The City's cross-complaint is dismissed. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Joint Notice Of Settlement And Motion For An Order For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement [C.C.P. 877.6(A)(2)]

The matter is argued. At conclusion of argument, the matter is taken under submission.
